entities.name: LENTIL
entities.description: Lens culinaris Medik., the crop studied for the limited-transpiration trait

entities.name: LIMITED-TRANSPIRATION
entities.description: A mechanism for water conservation under high vapor pressure deficit, also known as TRlim

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: An environmental factor that limits crop production

entities.name: HIGH VAPOR PRESSURE DEFICIT
entities.description: A condition (VPD) that triggers the limited-transpiration mechanism

entities.name: WATER CONSERVATION
entities.description: The process of saving water, resulting from the limited-transpiration trait

entities.name: YIELD INCREASE
entities.description: The improvement in crop output resulting from water conservation under water-deficit conditions

entities.name: WATER-DEFICIT CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental conditions characterized by a lack of available water

entities.name: WHOLE-PLANT MEASUREMENTS
entities.description: The technique used to observe the expression of the limited-transpiration trait

entities.name: CONTROLLED ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Settings where the expression of TRlim was compared against natural conditions

entities.name: NATURAL CONDITIONS
entities.description: Outdoor settings where the expression of TRlim was observed during high VPD periods

entities.name: VPD BREAKPOINT
entities.description: The specific vapor pressure deficit threshold at which the limited-transpiration trait is expressed

entities.name: SIMULATION ANALYSIS
entities.description: A computational approach used to identify the benefits of the TRlim trait across South Asia

entities.name: SOUTH ASIA
entities.description: The geographic region where the simulation analysis for lentil was conducted

entities.name: DROUGHT-PRONE ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Environments where the limited-transpiration trait at a low threshold improves lentil performance

entities.name: LENTIL PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The overall output of lentil, which varies based on geography, environment, and the TRlim trait
